name: ActiveCampaign
shortDescription: Connects FlowRunner to [ActiveCampaign](https://www.activecampaign.com) for email marketing, marketing automation, and CRM. Manage contacts, tags, lists, custom fields, deals, automations, campaigns, and notes, and react to account activity in real time. Built on the [ActiveCampaign v3 API](https://developers.activecampaign.com/reference).

actions.name: Sync Contact
actions.description: Creates a contact or updates the existing one matched by email address (upsert). Sets name, phone, and any custom field values in a single call. Use this as the default way to push contacts into ActiveCampaign without worrying about duplicates.

actions.name: Get Contact
actions.description: Retrieves a single contact by its ID, including email, name, phone, and timestamps. Use List Contacts first if you only know the email address.

actions.name: List Contacts
actions.description: Lists contacts with optional filtering by exact email, free-text search (name, email, phone, organization), and subscription status, plus paging. Returns the matching contacts and the total match count.

actions.name: Update Contact
actions.description: Updates an existing contact by ID. Only the fields you provide are changed; blank fields keep their current values. Use Sync Contact instead when you only know the email address.

actions.name: Delete Contact
actions.description: Permanently deletes a contact and its associated data from ActiveCampaign. This cannot be undone.

actions.name: Create Tag
actions.description: Creates a new contact tag in ActiveCampaign. Tags created here can then be applied to contacts with Add Tag To Contact.

actions.name: List Tags
actions.description: Lists tags in the account with optional name search and paging. Returns each tag's ID, name, type, and how many contacts carry it.

actions.name: Add Tag To Contact
actions.description: Applies an existing tag to a contact. Tagging can start automations that use "tag is added" as a trigger.

actions.name: Remove Tag From Contact
actions.description: Removes a tag from a contact. Looks up the contact's tag associations to find the matching one, then deletes it — you only need the contact ID and the tag, not the association ID.

actions.name: List Lists
actions.description: Lists the mailing lists in the account with optional exact-name filtering and paging. Returns each list's ID, name, and sender info.

actions.name: Update List Status For Contact
actions.description: Subscribes a contact to a mailing list or unsubscribes them from it. Subscribing an unconfirmed or previously unsubscribed contact re-adds them to the list.

actions.name: List Fields
actions.description: Lists the contact custom field definitions in the account — IDs, titles, types, and options. Use the returned field IDs when setting custom field values on contacts.

actions.name: Create Field Value
actions.description: Sets a custom field value on a contact. If the contact already has a value for the field it is replaced. For multi-value fields (checkbox, list box) use ||-delimited values, e.g. ||Option 1||Option 2||.

actions.name: Create Deal
actions.description: Creates a new deal in the CRM, placing it in a pipeline stage and optionally linking it to a contact and owner. The deal value is specified in cents (e.g. 45000 = $450.00).

actions.name: Get Deal
actions.description: Retrieves a single deal by its ID, including title, value, pipeline, stage, status, and linked contact.

actions.name: List Deals
actions.description: Lists deals with optional filtering by title search, pipeline, stage, status, and linked contact, plus paging. Returns the matching deals and the total match count.

actions.name: Update Deal
actions.description: Updates an existing deal by ID — title, value, currency, stage, owner, status, or description. Only the fields you provide are changed. Use the status field to mark a deal Won or Lost.

actions.name: List Pipelines
actions.description: Lists the deal pipelines (deal groups) in the account with paging. Use the returned IDs with Create Deal, Update Deal, and List Stages.

actions.name: List Stages
actions.description: Lists the stages of a deal pipeline in order. Use the returned stage IDs when creating, updating, or filtering deals.

actions.name: List Automations
actions.description: Lists the automations in the account with paging. Returns each automation's ID, name, status, and how many contacts have entered it.

actions.name: Add Contact To Automation
actions.description: Adds a contact to an automation, starting them at its first step. The contact enters the automation even if they don't meet its normal start-trigger conditions.

actions.name: List Campaigns
actions.description: Lists email campaigns with optional status filtering and paging. Each campaign includes basic performance stats such as send count, unique opens, and link clicks.

actions.name: Add Note To Contact
actions.description: Adds a note to a contact's activity timeline. Notes are visible on the contact record in ActiveCampaign.

idealUseCases: Sync leads captured elsewhere into ActiveCampaign as contacts without creating duplicates
Tag and segment contacts, then start them in automations based on external events
Keep CRM deals up to date and move them through pipeline stages
Subscribe or unsubscribe contacts on mailing lists and set custom field values
Trigger downstream workflows the moment a contact, tag, deal, or campaign event occurs
agentIdeas: When an **ActiveCampaign** "On ActiveCampaign Event" trigger fires for a new contact, use **Slack** "Send Message To Channel" to alert the sales team with the contact's email and name.
Use **Google Sheets** "Get Rows" to read a list of leads, then call **ActiveCampaign** "Sync Contact" for each row to upsert them into ActiveCampaign without duplicates.
When an **ActiveCampaign** "On ActiveCampaign Event" trigger fires for a won deal (Deal Updated), use **Gmail** "Send Message" to send the customer an onboarding welcome email.
